subject: Urology
title: Testicular Torsion: The 6-Hour Clock
hook: Sudden scrotal pain in a teenager is a clock you cannot stop. Move, or lose the testis.

points.heading: Why it twists
bullets: The cord twists and blocks venous outflow first
Then the artery is choked and the testis necroses
Classic setup: the bell clapper deformity

points.heading: A useful but imperfect sign
bullets: Cremasteric reflex is usually absent on that side
But a present reflex does NOT rule out torsion
Sudden severe pain, often nausea and vomiting

points.heading: Doppler confirms, never delays
bullets: Doppler shows absent central testicular flow
No scan or a doubtful picture means explore now
Imaging must never cost you the testis

points.heading: Fix both sides
bullets: Explore, detorse and orchidopexy the affected side
Always fix the other testis too, same anatomy
The 6-hour window decides if it survives

caption: You see a teenager with sudden, severe scrotal pain. The clock is already running. Salvage is near 100% inside 6 hours, then it falls off a cliff after 24. So when the cremasteric reflex is gone and the pain came on fast, that is theatre, not "wait for the scan." Miss the window and the testis is lost. Save this for your surgery rotation.
